Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Moss is a pixel canvas where every brush is a tiny program 236 upvotes, 25 comments
  2. NanoGPT Slowrun: Language Modeling with Limited Data, Infinite Compute 154 upvotes, 26 comments
  3. Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide 318 upvotes, 74 comments
  4. Building a new Flash 499 upvotes, 136 comments
  5. Relax NG is a schema language for XML 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. Libre Solar – Open Hardware for Renewable Energy 244 upvotes, 73 comments
  7. The View from RSS 108 upvotes, 28 comments
  8. “It turns out” (2010) 275 upvotes, 87 comments
  9. Show HN: Poppy – a simple app to stay intentional with relationships 67 upvotes, 17 comments
  10. You Just Reveived 87 upvotes, 24 comments
  11. Google Workspace CLI 449 upvotes, 164 comments
  12. Chaos and Dystopian news for the dead internet survivors 85 upvotes, 28 comments
  13. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 630 upvotes, 282 comments
  14. Raspberry Pi Pico as AM Radio Transmitter 90 upvotes, 31 comments
  15. Jensen Huang says Nvidia is pulling back from OpenAI and Anthropic 126 upvotes, 47 comments
  16. Picking Up a Zillion Pieces of Litter 93 upvotes, 38 comments
  17. Humans 40k yrs ago developed a system of conventional signs 106 upvotes, 48 comments
  18. Dario Amodei calls OpenAI’s messaging around military deal ‘straight up lies’ 482 upvotes, 267 comments
  19. Dulce et Decorum Est (1921) 114 upvotes, 63 comments
  20. NRC issues first commercial reactor construction approval in 10 years [pdf] 101 upvotes, 59 comments
  21. Malm Whale 24 upvotes, 10 comments
  22. You need to rewrite your CLI for AI agents 60 upvotes, 36 comments
  23. What Python's asyncio primitives get wrong about shared state 40 upvotes, 23 comments
  24. The Self-Help Trap: What 20 Years of "Optimizing" Has Taught Me 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  25. Was Windows 1.0's lack of overlapping windows a legal or a technical matter? 80 upvotes, 55 comments
  26. BMW Group to deploy humanoid robots in production in Germany for the first time 116 upvotes, 97 comments
  27. Relicensing with AI-Assisted Rewrite 64 upvotes, 51 comments
  28. MacBook Neo 1729 upvotes, 2023 comments
  29. AMD will bring its "Ryzen AI" processors to standard desktop PCs for first time 34 upvotes, 29 comments
  30. Zed new terms required to be 18 years old 21 upvotes, 27 comments

Last generated 3:30pm, 5 March 2026. Source here.
